dialogos.texto_en: *sigh*\nNo matter how many times I see it, it's still lovely.
(Is that Nino peeking into the shop window over there?\n She seems to be looking at it so intently...)
What are you looking at?
Ah, Fu-kun!
...Are you perhaps alone?
You can tell just by looking, can't you?
So, were you looking for me?
I just happened to see you.\nAnyway, what were you looking at?
...Ugh.\nIt's Ryusou, okay? Ryusou.
Look, it's traditional Okinawan clothing.\nThe vivid colors are so enchanting, don't you think?
Oh, this is 'bingata,' right?\nIt's a traditional Okinawan dyeing technique.
You know about it? You would, Fu-kun.
Hmm.\nThe patterns and colors\nseem a bit different from a normal kimono.
This is traditional Okinawan dyeing,\ncalled 'bingata.'
The colors and patterns are great, but really,\nthe design is just lovely.
Excuse me. Would you like to try it on?\nAt the moment, there are still some available,\nso it's okay.
Ah, sorry.\nI don't have much time...
I see. How about trying it on over your clothes,\nin that case?
You can still feel the atmosphere,\nand I'm sure it will look good on you.
If it's just that...
Do whatever you'd like.\nIf it's just putting it on,\nit shouldn't take too long, right?
You're right.\nThen, I'll try it on for a bit.
Alright, may I request this pattern, please?
Yes, of course.\nPlease come this way.
Fu-kun, don't peek, okay?
What are you talking about?\nIt's not like you're changing or anything.
You have this chance. Why not try it on?
Huh? Really?
You're just putting on a jacket, right?\nSurely, you have time for that.
Yes, you're right.\nMaybe I'll try it on for a bit.
Alright, may I request this pattern, please?
Yes, of course.\nPlease come this way.
Fu-kun, don't peek, okay?
What are you talking about?\nIt's not like you're changing or anything.
That's nice, isn't it?\nIt'd definitely look good on you, Nino.
Oh my? Does that mean you want to see me wear it?
Wh-what?\nThat's not really what I meant...
I'll show you as much of me as you want, Fu-kun.\nALL of it.
Stop saying things that could be misinterpreted.\nWe're in public.
Alright, may I request this pattern, please?
Yes, of course.\nPlease come this way.
Fu-kun, don't peek, okay?
What are you talking about?\nIt's not like you're changing or anything.
Hehe, just wait a bit, okay?
Fu-kun, thanks for waiting!
O-ohh...
How is it?
It suits you.
What? That's it?
What else should I say?
I want you to say something like 'cute' or 'beautiful,'\neven if it's just flattery.
Sorry, I'm not good at that kind of thing.
I know that, but can't you say it\nat least in times like these?
Actually, I'm not just flattering you.\nIt really does suit you.
Thank you, Fu-kun!
Ryukyu-style clothing is really beautiful,\nisn't it?\nI kind of want one for myself.
Did you like it?
We also offer photo services\nwith Ryukyu-style clothing\nand resort wedding plans here%p-2;――
How about using them for your future wedding?
Woah?!\nWait a minute.\nWe're not%p-2;――
Oh wow, why not?\nA resort wedding would be just lovely.
What are you talking about?\nYou're thinking way too far ahead.
...Hey?\nDoes that mean you're thinking\nabout marrying me in the future too?
Ah! No!\nThat's not what I meant when I said that...
Well, I believe in that future.
